text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little village nestled between the woods and the sea, there lived a curious fox named Felix. Every morning, Felix would hop down from his tall hat and strut along the cobblestone paths toward the old oak near the sea. It was hollow inside, filled with whispers.

One day, as Felix listened, he heard a little girl cry. She was lost. Felix scampered over, his tail wagging bravely. With a snicker and a hop, he gathered her up.

"Worry not, dear," said Felix. "Look where you are!"

The little girl giggled. "I've never been so close to the sea."

They walked together until the sun reached its highest point. Then Felix decided it was time to go home. But not before he gave her a tiny sea shell.

"I'll drop this at my mom's beach sometimes." She smiled.

Off she went. Felix scampered back home. That night, he dreamed of adventures.
